perturbation.original_text: PAYMENT TERMS All Parties agreed the payment listed below are fair and just for the services being provided. Payment to the following individual below within 7 days upon completion and handover on the Project to VAL.
perturbation.changed_text: PAYMENT TERMS All Parties agreed the payment listed below are fair and just for the services being provided. Payment to the following individual below within 60 days upon completion and handover on the Project to VAL.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that payment will be made within 7 days upon completion and handover. The changed text extends this to 60 days. This creates a direct contradiction with other clauses regarding project timelines and potential penalties for late payment that might be inferred from the original 7-day period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ESTIMATION AND COMMERCIALS Platform iOS, Android Development Chinese WeChat's Online Store, Social Media, Website, Online Promotion/Marketing and Online Payment for the P.R.China market. Total Delivery Time Min - 4 Calendar Months, Max - 6 Calendar Months Quotation (In USD) $ 200,000.00 Notes: Payments for this Agreement will be transferred by VAL via Bank Wire Transfer Method in US Dollars Currency as per Clause 5.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the payment term from 7 days to 60 days. This contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines a delivery timeline of 4-6 months and implies timely payment within the original 7-day timeframe to align with project milestones. The 60-day payment term could cause issues with VAL's cash flow and ability to meet financial obligations related to the project, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND OWNERSHIP All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by VAL, and will be turned over to VAL at the conclusion of the project by Developer and after the fulfillment of all commercial obligations by the VAL. All rights and title to Duesey Coffee Intellectual Property created pursuant to the Project shall belong to VAL and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND OWNERSHIP All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by the Developer, and will be licensed to VAL at the conclusion of the project by Developer and after the fulfillment of all commercial obligations by the VAL. All rights and title to Duesey Coffee Intellectual Property created pursuant to the Project shall belong to the Developer and shall be subject to a separate licensing agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that all Intellectual Property (IP) developed during the project is owned by VAL. The changed text states that the Developer owns the IP and will license it to VAL. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding IP ownership, potentially affecting clauses related to project deliverables, termination, and usage rights, as well as creating future conflict in what the actual final terms are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All rights and title to Duesey Coffee Intellectual Property created pursuant to the Project shall belong to VAL and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text by altering the ownership of intellectual property from VAL to the Developer. The original text states "All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by VAL", while the changed text asserts "All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by the Developer". This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding IP ownership, which is further highlighted by the contradictory statement that "All rights and title to Duesey Coffee Intellectual Property created pursuant to the Project shall belong to VAL" in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: TERMINATION VAL shall, in the event of Developer committing any breach of any of the terms and conditions of this agreement or for any other reason considered as sufficient, be entitled to terminate this agreement by giving two (2) weeks' notice in writing and it is applicable only when the project is not completed. If the customer terminates the agreement, then VAL shall compensate the Developer up to the date of termination with a fee calculated on Pro-rata basis.
perturbation.changed_text: TERMINATION VAL shall, in the event of Developer committing any breach of any of the terms and conditions of this agreement or for any other reason considered as sufficient, be entitled to terminate this agreement by giving two (2) weeks' notice in writing and it is applicable regardless of project completion status. If the customer terminates the agreement, then VAL shall compensate the Developer up to the date of termination with a fee calculated on a fixed fee basis of 50% of the remaining project value.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that VAL can terminate the agreement with two weeks' notice only when the project is not completed, and compensation is on a pro-rata basis. The modified text removes the restriction on project completion status and changes compensation to a fixed 50% of the remaining project value. This directly contradicts the original terms regarding when termination is allowed and how compensation is calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Developer may also terminate this Agreement by giving two (2) weeks' notice in writing to VAL. It is applicable only when the project is not completed. In case Developer terminates the agreement, it shall handover the entire project related IPR, work done till date to VAL.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the conditions under which VAL can terminate the agreement and the compensation structure upon termination. Originally, VAL could terminate only when the project was incomplete, and compensation was pro-rata. The modified text removes the project completion status restriction and changes compensation to a fixed 50% of the remaining project value. The contradicted text states that the Developer may terminate only when the project is not completed, and there is no mention of any such fixed compensation upon termination. This creates a clear contradiction because the developer termination conditions remain project incomplete and the modified VAL termination now has no project completion status, causing a contradiction on whether project completion status is a factor for termination. Also the change has implication with the contradicted text on the remedy because the developer termination makes no mention of compensation, so it creates a contradiction.
